Ad Text:
Archon - Reputable Tech Experts

·        Australian Federal Government
·        12-month contract with extension options (+12 months)
·        Australian residents only
·        Baseline clearance required, must be able to obtain an NV1 clearance
·        Hybrid role based in Canberra ACT. Essential office hours will be required
·        40 Hours per week
·        Response to the criteria will be required for this role (refer to criteria below)


Archon’s mission is to drive business growth, enable digital transformation, and strengthen security capabilities through innovative IT solutions.

Are you an experienced ERP Business Analyst looking for your next opportunity with one of Canberra’s leading consulting firms? Archon is currently seeking a Senior ERP Business Analyst to join our rapidly growing team, supporting a key government client.

Job details:

Archon is seeking a highly skilled and motivated Senior ERP Business Analyst that will provide senior level business analysis capability for our government departments ERP modernisation and associated transformation initiatives.

This role bridges business strategy, operational needs and ERP solution delivery. It is responsible for leading complex requirements analysis, business process design and solution definition to ensure the ERP platform delivers meaningful, compliant and sustainable business outcomes.

The role works closely with business owners, architects, delivery teams and system integrators to ensure requirements are well‑defined, prioritised, traceable and aligned to target‑state architecture and enterprise standards.


Key duties and responsibilities:

The ERP Senior Business Analyst is responsible for:

Business Analysis & Requirements

Leading end‑to‑end business analysis activities across ERP modules and processes

Eliciting, analysing and documenting complex business and functional requirements

Ensuring requirements align to business strategy and ERP standard capability

Managing requirements traceability throughout the delivery lifecycle


Business Process & Design

Analysing current‑state processes and designing efficient future‑state processes

Reducing customisation through process alignment to ERP best practice

Supporting configuration, test scenarios and user acceptance activities


Stakeholder Engagement

Engaging senior business stakeholders to agree priorities and outcomes

Translating complex business needs into clear, consumable artefacts

Supporting change, training and transition activities


Delivery Support & Assurance

Supporting solution design reviews and delivery governance forums

Managing scope, impacts and trade‑offs across ERP workstreams

Providing advice on risks, dependencies and implementation impacts


Essential

Significant experience as a senior business analyst on large ERP programs

Proven experience with ERP platforms (SAP, Workday, TechOne, Oracle, etc.)

Strong business process modelling and requirements management capability

Experience working in large, complex or government organisations

Excellent written and verbal communication skills

NV1 Security Clearance


Technical skills

Highly Desirable: 

Tertiary qualifications in business, ICT or related disciplines

BABOK or equivalent business analysis certification

Experience across finance, HR, payroll or grants ERP domains

Essential criteria

Candidate must provide a one-page pitch to address all criteria specified. This is equal to 5000 characters.

1. Business process improvement: Level 5 (SFIA)

Manages the execution of business process improvements. Analyses and designs business processes to identify alternative solutions to improve efficiency, effectiveness and exploit new technologies and automation. Develops graphical models of business processes to facilitate understanding and decision-making. Assesses the feasibility of business process changes and recommends alternative approaches. Selects, tailors and implements methods and tools for improving business processes at programme, project or team level. Contributes to the definition of organisational policies, standards, and guidelines for business process improvement.

2. Business situation analysis: Level 5 (SFIA)

Plans, manages and investigates business situation analysis where there is significant ambiguity and complexity. Advises on the approach and techniques to be used for business situation analysis. Ensures holistic view adopted to identify and analyse wide-ranging problems and opportunities. Engages and collaborates with a wide range of stakeholders, including those at the management level. Gains agreement from stakeholders to conclusions and recommendations. Contributes to definition of organisational standards and guidelines for business situation analysis.

3. Feasibility assessment: Level 5 (SFIA)

Manages investigative work to enable feasibility assessments. Collaborates with stakeholders and specialists to get the information required for feasibility assessment. Advises on the selection of feasibility assessment approaches and techniques relevant to the business situation and options. Prepares business cases, including cost/benefit, impact and risk analysis for each option.

4. Requirement’s definition and management: Level 5 (SFIA)

Plans and drives scoping, requirements definition and prioritisation activities for large, complex initiatives. Selects, adopts, and adapts appropriate requirements definition and management methods, tools and techniques. Contributes to the development of organisational methods and standards for requirements management. Obtains input from, and agreement to requirements from a diverse range of stakeholders. Negotiates with stakeholders to manage competing priorities and conflicts. Establishes requirements baselines. Ensures changes to requirements are investigated and managed.


5. Stakeholder relationship management: Level 5 (SFIA)

Identifies the communications and relationship needs of stakeholder groups. Translates communications/stakeholder engagement strategies into specific activities and deliverables. Facilitates open communication and discussion between stakeholders. Acts as a single point of contact by developing, maintaining and working to stakeholder engagement strategies and plans. Provides informed feedback to assess and promote understanding. Facilitates business decision-making processes. Captures and disseminates technical and business information.

More about us:

www.archonsolutions.com.au

A well-established manufacturing company, is seeking an experienced Pricing and Performance Manager to lead and strengthen its pricing capability.

This role will be responsible for pricing governance, pricing data maintenance, and pricing implementation across business systems, while also delivering commercial performance analysis to optimise profitability and competitiveness across the company's product portfolio.

The successful candidate will bring strong analytical capability, clear communication skills, and the ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams across the business.

Key Responsibilities:

Develop and execute pricing strategies to maximise revenue, margin, and competitiveness across products and services.
Own pricing data governance and implementation across business systems and tools.
Lead financial modelling, business planning, and divisional performance reporting.
Manage commercial and contractual governance, ensuring compliance with policies and approvals.
Conduct market and competitor analysis to inform pricing decisions.
Support customer negotiations through commercial modelling, contract pricing, and pricing policy guidance.
Build models to support product launches, commercialisation, and sales initiatives.
Provide sales, margin, and COGS analysis across the business.
Monitor pricing performance against targets, identify opportunities, and recommend improvements.
Partner with Sales, Marketing, and Product teams to align pricing with market demand and business goals.
Support pricing negotiations with customers and vendors to achieve sustainable outcomes.
Develop and maintain pricing tools, models, and processes to improve decision-making.
Ensure divisional pricing activities meet legal, regulatory, and internal governance requirements.
Keep abreast of industry trends and best practice in pricing and performance management.

Qualifications and Experience:

Bachelor's degree in business, commerce, finance, economics, accounting, or a related field. Master's degree is advantages but not essential.
Lubricants or FMCG experience advantages.
Proven experience in pricing strategy, financial analysis, and performance management.
Experience in using PROS, Flintfox or equivalent pricing tool highly regarded.
Experience in developing price management strategy
Demonstrable experience in successfully introducing tiered price strategies into a
Experience in a pricing role within consumer durable and/or industrial B2B market environment
Experience in developing and optimising pricing processes to drive improvement in sales and marketing productivity.
Demonstrated commitment to working within an established code of conduct.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking for a pricing role they can truly make their own. The successful applicant will have the autonomy to shape pricing strategy, review future business requirements, and help set the direction of pricing capability moving forward.

We are currently recruiting for an experienced Financial Planning & Analysis Manager to join a values‑driven, not‑for‑profit organisation during a period of significant uplift and transformation. Reporting to the Financial Controller, this new created position will be responsible for embedding financial planning, forecasting and performance insight to support confident decision‑making and long‑term financial sustainability.

About the Role

This position sits at the connection of finance, strategy and performance, providing a clear, independent view of the organisation’s rolling financial outlook, cash flow position and affordability. You will work closely with senior leaders and planning stakeholders across a complex environment, translating strategy and initiatives into credible financial forecasts, scenarios and trade‑offs.

Key responsibilities of the role include:

Owning a rolling financial forecast across P&L, balance sheet and cash flow
Providing forward‑looking insight on affordability, liquidity and capital needs
Translating strategy and initiatives into clear financial impacts and scenarios
Supporting executive decisions through analysis, trade‑offs and prioritisation
Simplifying and strengthening budgeting, forecasting and planning processes
Partnering across the organisation while leading a small specialist FP&A team
About You

Some of the skills and experience you will bring to this position will include:

CA or CPA qualified with a relevant tertiary qualification
Proven experience in FP&A, management accounting or financial planning roles
Strong capability in rolling forecasts, cash flow modelling and scenario analysis
Experience uplifting planning capability and improving financial processes
Ability to translate complex financial information into clear, decision‑useful insights
Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing skills in complex or decentralised environments
Experience in the not‑for‑profit or community services sector highly regarded
This role will suit an individual who enjoys shaping future-facing capability, influencing outcomes, and supporting purpose‑led organisations with strong financial discipline.

If this role is of interest to you and you feel you have the desired experience, please apply by submitting your application or for further information, please email Jack Green at Troocoo - jack.green@troocoo.com or call on 0472 707 645.

Job Title: Vice President, Fixed Asset Advisory Services
Ad Text:
In a world of disruption and increasingly complex business challenges, our professionals bring truth into focus with the Kroll Lens. Our sharp analytical skills, paired with the latest technology, allow us to give our clients clarity—not just answers— in all areas of business. We embrace diverse backgrounds and global perspectives, and we cultivate diversity by respecting, including, and valuing one another. As part of One team, One Kroll, you’ll contribute to a supportive and collaborative work environment that empowers you to excel. 

Our Fixed Asset Advisory Services (FAAS) practice provides fixed asset valuations to a diverse portfolio of global clients.

At Kroll, your work will help deliver clarity to our clients’ most complex governance, risk, and transparency challenges. Apply now to join One team, One Kroll. 

RESPONSIBILITIES:

1. Global methodology & cost alignment

Drive alignment and standardisation of valuation and cost modelling methodologies across multiple geographies, business units and engagement types.

Reconcile and consolidate disparate cost-related data sources that exist across the business, establishing a single, coherent framework for cost benchmarking that aligns with global requirements.


2. Tech-enabled process automation 

Lead the design, development and deployment of AI-enabled tools that automate and accelerate the valuation process modelling lifecycle, from data ingestion and cost estimation through to final reporting.

Work cross-functionally with Kroll's technology and data teams (with technical support available) to specify requirements, test prototypes and scale proven tools across the practice.

Leverage generative AI, machine learning and advanced analytics to embed intelligence into valuation workflows, reducing manual effort and enhancing analytical rigour.

Champion a culture of digital innovation within the Fixed Assets practice, identifying opportunities where technology can materially improve quality, consistency and profitability of engagements.


3. Data Strategy & Structured Dataset Development

Unlock the value of Kroll's extensive historical engagement dataset by leading the conversion of unstructured asset data — accumulated across prior mandates globally — into clean, structured and queryable datasets.

Integrate structured datasets into active valuation engagements to improve cost benchmarking, market-rate assumptions and analytical depth, reducing reliance on external data sources.

Identify patterns, trends and insights from aggregated engagement data to support knowledge development, proposal writing and thought leadership.


4. People leadership & capability building

Upskilling team members by driving tech-enablement across the business 

REQUIREMENTS:

Significant experience in valuation, fixed assets, cost modelling or a related advisory discipline.

Demonstrated track record of leading process improvement, automation or data‑led transformation initiatives.

Strong commercial mindset with the ability to link technology initiatives to margin, efficiency and scalability outcomes.

Experience working across regions and influencing senior stakeholders without direct authority.

Comfort working alongside technology teams, with the ability to translate business requirements into practical, scalable solutions.

Recognized technical expertise in valuation or within a specific industry vertical; ability to communicate complex technical issues to senior executives.

Strong people leadership skills with a track record of coaching, team development, and fostering collaborative culture.

High level of integrity and professionalism

Commitment and aptitude to grow our business and brand

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with maturity and confidence presenting to senior stakeholders.

About Kroll 

Join the global leader in risk and financial advisory solutions—Kroll. With a nearly century-long legacy, we blend trusted expertise with cutting-edge technology to navigate and redefine industry complexities. As a part of One Team, One Kroll, you'll contribute to a collaborative and empowering environment, propelling your career to new heights. Ready to build, protect, restore and maximize our clients’ value? Your journey begins with Kroll. 

Kroll is committed to equal opportunity and diversity, and recruits people based on merit. 

In order to be considered for a position, you must formally apply via careers.kroll.com

Ad Text:
The role 

Reporting to the Director, Service Management and improvement, the Reporting and Analysis Manager is responsible for delivering customer insights to eHealth Queensland staff and identifying performance insights that promote end-to-end improvement in eHealth Queensland's service delivery to its customers.

The Reporting and Analysis Manager leads a specialist team of Business Intelligence and Performance Reporting experts, managing and setting the direction of current and future activities. The manager adds value through extensive knowledge of Business Intelligence and Performance Reporting, coupled with extensive knowledge of the operations of eHealth Queensland and its customers, to implement value-adding performance reporting and analysis solutions based on industry standards and best practices. They are expected to possess leadership skills to bring together multi-disciplined teams to embed performance reporting and analysis into service operations as well as providing peer mentoring, guidance and internal development. The role will work broadly across the Enterprise Technology Services branch plus other areas of eHealth, with varied stakeholders and customers.

Key responsibilities:

Manage the core eHealth Queensland customer intelligence and service performance reporting and analysis operational processes ensuring that they meet the needs of eHealth Queensland's customers and support teams
Measure and report outcomes and performance against defined metrics
Optimise toolsets to standardise and automate processes as much as possible
Lead and manage a team of Business Intelligence and Performance Reporting specialists, instilling a culture of customer-first, continual improvement and analytical best practice and innovation
Develop and maintain the framework for service delivery performance reporting and analysis, ensuring that the process provides trustworthy data and timely delivery of information and analysis to reduce the delays in decision-making processes caused by complex or disparate manual processes, and provides end-to-end insight across eHealth Queensland's services

About you

We are searching for someone who:

Demonstrated knowledge and experience of the service management capabilities of ServiceNow, the SQL Server toolset as it supports best practice data management, specifically in a data warehousing environment, and Power BI to provide information that supports effective decision-making.
Demonstrated high level understanding of Business Intelligence and Performance Reporting frameworks and best practice, as they apply to a large ICT customer service delivery organisation
Demonstrated ability to design, implement and promote effective Business Intelligence and Performance Reporting frameworks and solutions within a large, complex service delivery organisation
Advanced interpersonal, communication, negotiation, consultation and collaboration skills incorporating the ability to liaise and consult effectively with personnel at all levels within and external to eHealth Queensland
Demonstrated high level organisational skills including the ability to establish own work priorities and manage time effectively, whilst remaining flexible to respond to changing needs in a busy environment
High level oral and written communication skills, including the ability to compile complex reports, briefs, correspondence and submissions on behalf of the Director, Service Management and Improvement
Demonstrated high level research, analytical, conceptual and problem solving skills including the proven capacity to initiate appropriate strategic and operational responses to key issues affecting the enterprise
Highly developed stakeholder engagement and management skills with the ability to build strong relationships with both internal and external stakeholders
Demonstrated ability to lead, manage and mentor staff committed to the delivery of a diverse and dynamic work program to achieve positive service outcomes for customers while effectively managing conflicting demands and tight time frames

Ready to apply?

For further information on how to apply please review the attached Role Description.
